title: turnera iterata
intro: Turnera iterata, also known as the West Indian sarsaparilla or prickly sarsaparilla, is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the Passifloraceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Turnera iterata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate arthritis symptoms, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
